question.text: Under the SEBI Investment Advisers Regulations, individual Investment Advisers:

answer.text: Can simultaneously provide advisory and distribution services for the same client
Cannot undertake both advisory and distribution — they must choose one
Must mandatorily provide execution services along with advice
Are exempt from registration if they serve fewer than 10 clients

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under amendments to the IA Regulations, SEBI has stated that individual Investment Advisers cannot undertake both advisory and distribution. They must register as either an adviser or a distributor. **The Trap:** Non-individual IAs can do both with client-level segregation, but individual IAs cannot. **Cross-Exam Context:** SEBI IA Regulations are heavily tested in X-A (Ch 18) and X-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Choose your business model carefully — the advisory-distribution separation is strictly enforced by SEBI.
